OkaySpace Game Engine — Changelog

v2.16.21
  - Character Creator: animation! Characters can now move.
    * Animation selector — None / Idle (gentle sway) / Walk / Run — with an
      Anim Speed slider. Plays in Play mode: arms and legs swing fore/aft
      (arms counter the legs) for a natural gait.
    * Engine: Humanoid() supports antisymmetric arm/leg swing; CharacterBody
      animates it per-frame during play. Animation choice + speed save/load.

v2.16.20
  - Character Creator: accessories.
    * Hat (brim + crown) and Glasses (sunglasses bar) toggles, each with its own
      color.
    * Save/load, back-compatible.

v2.16.19
  - Character Creator: more facial detail.
    * Ears (toggle), pupils inside the eyes, an adjustable Mouth Width (smile),
      and a Brow Angle slider (angry/neutral/worried expressions).
    * All new fields save/load (back-compatible).

v2.16.18
  - Character Creator: hair styles + eye spacing.
    * Hair Style selector: Cap, Short, Long (back panel), Spiky, Ponytail,
      Mohawk.
    * Eye Spacing slider to widen/narrow the eyes.
    * Both save/load (back-compatible).

v2.16.17
  - Character Creator: faces and a full outfit.
    * A simple face — eyes (with their own Eye color), brows, nose, and mouth —
      added to the head; toggle it with "Face".
    * Outfit split into Skin / Shirt / Pants / Shoes color regions (torso+arms =
      shirt, hips+legs = pants, feet = shoes) for proper clothed characters.
    * All new colors + the Face toggle save/load (back-compatible).

v2.16.16
  - Character Creator: multi-color characters.
    * Separate Skin, Outfit, and Hair colors (head/neck/hands = skin;
      torso/arms/legs/feet = outfit), plus an optional Hair cap on the head.
    * New engine support: meshes can carry per-triangle colors; the software
      renderer (editor + player) honors them, and they survive subdivision so
      colors stay crisp from low-poly to high-poly.
    * Colors save/load and are included in .OBJ-exported geometry's shape.

v2.16.15
  - Character Creator: more anatomy + export.
    * Torso Length (raises the upper body, keeping limbs attached) and Body Depth
      (front-to-back thickness) sliders.
    * Export .OBJ — bake the character (at its current detail level) to
      assets/<name>.obj for reuse as a regular model asset.
    * Randomize now also varies torso length and body depth.
    * New fields save/load (back-compatible).

v2.16.14
  - Character Creator expanded:
    * New proportions: Hand Size and Foot Size.
    * Posing: Arm Spread (A-pose/T-pose) and Leg Spread (stance) angle the
      limbs out from the body (limbs now rotate about the shoulder/hip).
    * More presets: Masculine, Feminine, Child, Robot (plus Reset/Slim/Heavy/Tall).
    * Randomize button generates a random body in one click.
    * All new fields save/load (back-compatible with v2.16.13 character data).

v2.16.13
  - Character Creator component (GameObject > 3D Object > Character):
    * Live proportion sliders — Height, Build, Head Size, Shoulder/Hip Width,
      Arm/Leg Length, Neck Length — rebuild the humanoid as you drag.
    * Detail sliders take it from low-poly to high-poly (Subdivisions 0-3 +
      Smoothness), with a live triangle counter.
    * Quick presets: Reset, Slim, Heavy, Tall; plus a skin Color.
    * Full save/load: a "character" component persists all parameters and
      rebuilds its mesh on load (no baked geometry stored).

v2.16.12
  - Procedural human character + low-poly to high-poly workflow:
    * New "Human (low-poly)" primitive (GameObject > 3D Object > Human, and the
      Hierarchy create menu) — a humanoid blockout built from body-part shapes.
    * Mesh Renderer gets a "Subdiv+Smooth" button that quadruples the triangles
      and relaxes them (Laplacian smoothing), turning the blocky low-poly human
      into smooth high-poly geometry. Click it repeatedly for more detail.
    * Engine: Mesh::Humanoid(), Mesh::Smooth(amount), Mesh::SubdivideSmooth(),
      and Mesh::Add() for compound meshes.

v2.16.11
  - Command Palette in the Script Editor (Ctrl+Shift+P, or the new "Cmds"
    button): type to fuzzy-search and run editor actions — Run, Save, Format,
    Reload, toggle Syntax/Minimap, comment line, zoom, open docs/IDE — and it
    also lists every function/class so you can "Go to" any symbol.

v2.16.10
  - Script Editor minimap (VS Code-style): a scaled overview of the whole file
    pinned beside the editor. Each line is a bar (length ~ its content, comments
    tinted green), a translucent box marks the visible region, and you can click
    or drag the minimap to scroll. Toggle it with the new "Map" checkbox.

v2.16.9
  - Inline compile diagnostics in the Script Editor:
    * OkayScript parse/compile errors now report the source line (e.g.
      "line 12: parse error: expected '}'").
    * The offending line gets a red wavy underline in the editor.
    * The error in the status bar is clickable — jump straight to the line.

v2.16.8
  - Script Editor visual polish (VS Code-style):
    * Bracket-pair colorization: matching ()[]{} are tinted by nesting depth
      (gold / magenta / blue) so you can read nesting at a glance.
    * Indent guides: faint vertical lines mark each 4-space indent level.
    * Highlight all occurrences of the identifier under the caret across the
      whole file.

v2.16.7
  - Script Editor gained more VS Code-style tools:
    * Modified (dot) marker on tabs and in the status bar when a script has
      unsaved changes; it clears on Save/Reload.
    * Format button re-indents the whole document by brace depth (4 spaces
      per level), skipping braces inside strings and // comments.
    * Outline button lists the file's functions and classes; pick one to jump
      and scroll straight to it.
    * Find Next / Prev arrows (and F3 / Shift+F3) step through matches around
      the caret, selecting and scrolling to each.
    * Status bar now shows the selection length, e.g. "Col 7 (12 sel)".

v2.16.6
  - Hierarchy panel gained Unity-style touches:
    * Expand All / Collapse All buttons to open or close the whole tree at once.
    * Sort toggle (A-Z) that alphabetically orders siblings and roots.
    * Component icon badges at the right of each row (S=Script, L=Light,
      R=Rigidbody, C=Collider, A=Animator, P=Particles) for a quick read of
      what's attached — like Unity's component icons.
    * Child-count shown on parents, e.g. "Player (3)".
    * Badges also appear in search results.

v2.16.5
  - Rotate gizmo is richer and more Unity-like: rings now fade on their
    camera-facing back half (so they read as a 3D sphere), the ring under the
    cursor highlights before you grab it, a translucent wedge + live degree
    readout show how far you're turning, and a new outer screen-aligned ring
    lets you free-spin about the camera/view axis.
  - Autocomplete now understands member access: typing "transform." lists
    position, rotation, Translate, Rotate, …; likewise Input., Time., Mathf.,
    Vector3., Physics2D., Debug., Random., Quaternion., Color., SceneManager.,
    Screen. and more. Plain keyword/builtin completion is unchanged.
  - You can now edit Project scripts directly: double-click a .okay/.lua/.cs in
    the Project window to open it in the in-app Script Editor as its own tab
    (no GameObject required). Save/Run/Reload work as usual.

v2.16.4
  - Rotate tool is now Unity-style: instead of straight axis arms, the Rotate
    gizmo (E) draws three colored rings (X/Y/Z) around the selection. Grab a ring
    and drag tangent to it to spin about that axis. Snap detents still apply.
  - Project window now draws real category icons instead of plain letter chips:
    folders, script pages, material spheres, scene/prefab hexes, audio, and
    meshes each get a distinct drawn shape in their category color.
  - Fixed drag-and-drop from the Project window into the Inspector replacing the
    existing script: dropping a .okay now always ATTACHES a new Script component,
    so you can stack as many as you want (matching Add Component).
  - Fixed an ImGui "ID conflict" popup when hovering "Open in Script Editor" (and
    other widgets) on an object that has more than one script — each script's
    inspector widgets are now uniquely scoped.

v2.16.3
  - Fixed adding multiple scripts: you can now attach as many scripts as you like
    to one object — including the SAME script more than once (Unity allows it) —
    via Add Component or drag-and-drop. The earlier "can't attach twice" block is
    gone.
  - Public variables are more editable, Unity-style: Vector3/Vector2 fields edit
    as three numbers, [Range(a,b)] shows a slider, [Header("..")] adds a section,
    and [Tooltip("..")] shows on hover. Numbers/strings/bools as before.
  - The inspector now reads the script you're TYPING (the live editor buffer), so
    new public vars appear immediately — no need to press Run (Run executes the
    script; Save just writes the file).

v2.16.2
  - Multiple scripts per GameObject (like Unity): Add Component > Scripts no
    longer replaces the existing script — each adds another, and the Inspector
    shows every Script with its own header and public variables. The same script
    file can't be attached twice. Script Editor tabs are now per-script, and
    scenes serialize all of an object's scripts (with their field overrides).
  - Only PUBLIC variables show in the inspector now: a var must be marked
    `public` (or `[SerializeField]`) to appear; an undecorated or `private` var
    is treated as private and hidden.

v2.16.1
  - Script inspector: the variable NAME is now on the left and the value on the
    right (Unity layout). Overridden values are highlighted, and the type shows
    on hover.

v2.16.0
  - Script inspector is now Unity-style: gone are the language dropdown, file
    path and "edit in Script Editor" note. Instead it shows the script's PUBLIC
    VARIABLES as editable fields (top-level / public vars like "public float
    speed = 5;"), with a per-object value that overrides the script default, a
    revert button, and an "Open in Script Editor" button.
  - Under the hood: scripts gained per-instance field overrides — the VM can set
    globals, ScriptComponent stores the overrides and applies them over the
    script's defaults after each compile, and they serialize with the scene.

v2.15.4
  - Project panel improvements: a type filter (Scripts/Images/Scenes/Materials/
    Prefabs/Data/Audio), a sort mode (Name/Type), an adjustable thumbnail size,
    Duplicate and Copy Path in the right-click menu, and a footer showing the
    item count plus the selected asset's name and size.

v2.15.3
  - Fixed: dropping a script onto an already-selected object's Inspector now
    opens its Script Editor tab right away (the tab only opened on a selection
    change before). Script drops also reuse an existing Script component and
    refresh the editor's buffer, so the dropped file shows immediately.
  - Script editor autocomplete: as you type an identifier, a suggestion list of
    keywords / API / builtins pops up at the caret — click one to insert it.
  - Scripting reference updated with the save()/load() system (in-editor Help >
    Scripting Reference and docs/scripting.md).

v2.15.2
  - Script editor tabs: edit several scripts at once. Selecting an object with a
    Script opens (and focuses) a tab for it; switch between open scripts with the
    tab bar, reorder them, and close one with its X. The editor (and all its
    tools) act on the active tab, so it no longer follows the current selection.

v2.15.1
  - Script editor, more IDE features: auto-closing brackets and quotes with
    type-over, auto-indent on Enter (matches the line, adds a level after "{"),
    a toggle for it ("Auto"), bracket-match highlighting (boxes the pair around
    the caret), and Find & Replace (a Replace field + Replace All).

v2.15.0
  - Keyframe Animator: the engine's property animator (AnimationClip of
    position/rotation/scale curves) is now usable in the editor. Add it from
    Add Component > Animation > Animator, then play/pause, scrub the timeline,
    and "Record Key" to capture the object's transform into tracks at the current
    time. Per-track key counts with Clear; clips save with the scene.
  - Script editor, even more: duplicate the current line with Ctrl+D, move it
    with Alt+Up/Alt+Down, and insert templates from the new Snippets menu
    (start/update/class/if/for/foreach/while/save-load/...). These run through the
    editor's input callback, so they work on the live buffer.
  - Docs & README refreshed to cover the save system, keyframe animation, the
    VS Code-style script editor, Unity-style Add Component, autosave/recovery,
    Material/Save Manager windows, and the expanded camera/lighting.

v2.14.4
  - Safer autosave / crash recovery: instead of silently overwriting your scene,
    autosave now writes a "<scene>.autosave" recovery copy (on by default, every
    2 min, configurable under File > Autosave). If the editor didn't close
    cleanly, opening the scene offers to Recover the newer copy or Discard it. A
    normal Save removes the recovery copy.

v2.14.3
  - Script editor: toggle a line comment with the "//" button or Ctrl+/, and jump
    to a line with the Go box (the view scrolls to center it). These act on the
    live buffer via the editor's input callback, so they work while you're typing.

v2.14.2
  - New Save Manager window (Window > Save Manager): browse the .okaysave files
    in your project (and the default save), view and edit their keys in a typed
    table, add/remove keys, clear or re-save a file. The editor counterpart to
    the runtime save() / load() system — inspect or tweak exactly what a playtest
    wrote.

v2.14.1
  - Script editor: the current line is now highlighted (VS Code-style), the
    status bar shows a live character count, and you can zoom the code with the
    A- / A+ buttons or Ctrl + mouse wheel (the gutter and syntax overlay stay
    aligned at any zoom).

v2.14.0
  - Lighting expanded: lights can be tinted by color temperature (Kelvin, e.g.
    2700K warm to 12000K cool), spot cones gained a Softness control for
    feathered edges, and the ambient floor can be a color tint, not just a
    brightness. Multi-light shading uses the colored ambient and a smooth spot
    falloff.
  - Camera expanded: Clear Flags (Skybox vs Solid Color), exposed Near/Far
    clipping planes, a render Depth/priority, and a full inspector. A Solid-Color
    camera suppresses the skybox in the Game view.
  - New Easy-Save-style save system: save()/load() with typed values (number,
    string, Vector3), many named files (slots/profiles), write-through to disk,
    plus save_has / save_delete / save_clear / save_exists / save_delete_file.
    Documented in the scripting reference.
  - Script editor additions: a Find bar (Ctrl+F) that highlights every match with
    a count, and the last compile error is shown inline (red) in the status bar.
  - Project/Inspector polish: drop a script or material anywhere in the Inspector
    (not just the header) to attach/apply it; rename assets with F2; creating a
    script/material/scene/folder immediately prompts for a name; and both panels
    got roomier spacing (they were too compact).

v2.13.0
  - Script editor reworked to feel like VS Code: dark editor surface, a
    line-number gutter, live syntax highlighting (keywords, types, strings,
    comments, numbers), a toolbar (Run / Save / Reload / Open in IDE / Docs /
    Syntax toggle) and a status bar (language, Ln/Col, line count). Tab inserts
    in code.
  - Add Component now matches Unity: a centered "Component" title, a Search box,
    and categories that drill into submenus while browsing (flat filtered list
    while searching). The Scripts category lists every .okay script in your
    project directly (one click attaches it) plus "New Script...".
  - Drag a script/material/image from the Project panel onto the Inspector's
    object header to attach it (script -> Script component, material -> applied,
    image -> texture).
  - Scripts use OkaySpace's own base class, OkaySource, instead of MonoBehaviour
    (Unity's ": MonoBehaviour" still parses, so pasted scripts keep working).
  - Add Component > Script now works like Unity: it opens a submenu that lists
    every .okay script already in your project's Assets (pick one to attach it),
    plus a "New Script..." entry that prompts for a name, creates the .okay file
    under Assets, and attaches it ("Create and Add"). Adding a script always
    references a real file now.
  - Inspector parity with Unity: every component header now has an enable/disable
    checkbox and a right-click > Remove Component menu. The Tag field became a
    dropdown (Untagged/Player/MainCamera/Enemy/... plus custom), and the object
    header gained a Static toggle that's saved with the scene.
  - Drag & drop into the Scene view: drag an asset from the Project panel onto the
    viewport to place it — prefabs instantiate, scenes open, images become
    sprites, and .obj models become 3D objects (dropped at the camera's focus).
  - Hierarchy: inactive objects are dimmed (Unity-style), and a new right-click
    "Create Parent" wraps an object in a fresh empty parent without moving it.
  - Add Component > "New Script..." creates a starter .okay file under Assets and
    attaches it in one step. Project panel gained "+ Material" and "+ Scene"
    create buttons (and matching right-click entries).
  - Scene view: light gizmos now show their reach — a range sphere for point
    lights and a cone for spot lights (directional keeps its sun arrow), tinted
    by each light's color. The corner overlay shows live FPS, and a new "Align
    Cam to View" button snaps a selected camera to the editor viewpoint (Unity's
    Align With View).
  - Snapping now covers all three transform tools: Move snaps to the grid
    increment, Scale snaps to it too, and Rotate snaps in configurable degree
    detents (default 15 deg) via a new "Rot deg" field in the Scene toolbar.
  - Local/Global gizmo toggle (Unity-style, shortcut X): handles can follow the
    object's own orientation or the world axes; Scale always uses local axes so
    scaling a rotated object behaves correctly.
  - Camera Preview: selecting a perspective Camera shows a live inset in the
    Scene view of exactly what that camera sees (Unity's Camera Preview), so you
    can frame shots without switching to the Game view.
  - Material inspector: double-click a .okaymat asset to edit its albedo,
    emissive, specular/shininess, unlit/double-sided, texture and tiling, then
    Save or "Apply to Selection" to stamp it onto the selected mesh. You can also
    drag a .okaymat from the Project straight onto an object in the Hierarchy to
    apply it, or drag an image onto an object to set its texture.

v2.12.0
  - New Project Settings window (Edit > Project Settings...): set your game's
    company and version, default resolution, 2D/3D gravity, and ambient/skybox
    rendering defaults in one place. Settings persist to project.okayproj, can
    be applied to the open scene live, and "Use in Build" pushes them straight
    into the build dialog.
  - More build settings — a new "Gameplay" section in Build lets you control
    how the finished game behaves: Quit on Escape (toggle whether Esc closes the
    game), Master Volume (scale all audio 0–100%), and Show FPS overlay (a small
    on-screen frame-rate readout). These are written into game.okayconfig and
    honored by the player, so each built game can be customized without code.

v2.11.1
  - UI looked bloated/"weird": v2.10's spacing bump was too aggressive (very tall
    fields and toolbar). Restored balanced, professional metrics (close to the
    long-proven values — comfortable but not bloated), and the global UI scale is
    now applied after all metrics so everything scales together. View > UI Scale
    still lets you size the whole interface up.

v2.11.0
  - Lighting overhauled: multiple lights now contribute at once, in color, with
    three types — Directional (sun), Point (radiates to a Range), and Spot (a
    cone with a Spot Angle). Each light has Type, Color, Intensity, Ambient,
    Range and Spot Angle in the inspector, and there are GameObject > Create
    Point Light / Spot Light entries. Scenes serialize the new fields (backward
    compatible). Scripts' set_light/set_ambient still drive the look when there's
    no Light object.
  - Fixed the editor UI looking stretched/blurry: the 110% default scaled the
    bitmap font at a non-integer ratio. The default is back to crisp 100% (the
    roomier spacing stays), and View > UI Scale still lets you size it up.

v2.10.0
  - Less-compact editor UI: roomier spacing/padding and a new View > UI Scale
    (Compact / Normal / Comfortable / Large / Huge) that scales the whole
    interface (font + metrics). Defaults to a comfortable 110%.
  - Expanded Build Settings: product Version, a Borderless window option, Hide
    cursor, a Frame-rate cap (Uncapped/30/60/120/144), and a 2560x1440 resolution
    preset — all written to game.okayconfig and applied by the player runtime.

v2.9.0
  - Hierarchy rearranging (Unity-style): DRAG a row and drop near another row's
    top/bottom edge to reorder it as a sibling (a blue insert line shows where),
    or drop onto the middle to re-parent. Right-click adds Move Up / Move Down /
    Move to Top / Move to Bottom, and Ctrl+Up / Ctrl+Down reorder the selection.
  - You can now select the Camera (and Lights / empty objects) in the Scene view
    by clicking their gizmo — non-visual objects no longer need a renderer to be
    picked.

v2.8.2
  - Fixed the in-app updater "doesn't update": the published editor binary was
    built one version behind VERSION.txt, so after updating the version never
    advanced and the same update kept being offered. The binaries are now built
    AT the released version (they match VERSION.txt), and the updater rejects a
    cached HTML/error page (validates the downloaded file is a real .exe) before
    swapping it in.

v2.8.1
  - Fixed 3D primitives rendering "inside-out": the Cube, Pyramid and Wedge had
    clockwise (inward-facing) winding, so backface culling dropped their front
    faces and you saw the interior. Now wound counter-clockwise (outward) like
    Plane/Sphere/Cylinder — they render solid in the Scene view and built game.
  - 3D objects now default to a neutral light gray (like Unity/Blender) instead
    of cyan/yellow, so new primitives look clean and shade naturally.

v2.8.0
  - Text component expanded: letter spacing, line spacing, UPPERCASE, and word
    wrap (to the box), on top of the existing align / box / shadow / outline /
    bold / background options. Multi-line renders consistently in editor + game.
  - Button component expanded: smooth color transitions (Transition Speed),
    per-state Hover Text color, a Press Offset "push", Icon-on-right, Toggle Button
    mode (stays on, flips isOn each click), and Hold-to-Repeat (steppers/spinners
    with Repeat Delay + Rate). All exposed in the inspector and serialized.
  - Hierarchy reordering: Move Up / Move Down in a node's right-click menu and
    Ctrl+Up / Ctrl+Down reorder the selected object among its siblings (children
    reorder within their parent; roots among roots) — drives UI draw order too.

v2.7.0
  - Crash fixes:
      * Deleting ANY UI widget (or any child object) no longer crashes. Destroying
        a GameObject now detaches it from its parent's child list and destroys its
        whole subtree, so no dangling Transform pointers are left behind.
      * Removing a component mid-frame is now iterator-safe (active list nulls the
        slot and compacts between frames).
      * Hardened against malformed scene data: ParticleSystem clamps maxParticles,
        Tilemap guards divide-by-zero on tileSize, and the script parser
        bounds-checks generic-call lookahead (fixes a possible out-of-bounds read).
  - Editor fixes:
      * Rename popup (double-click a hierarchy row) now works — OK/Cancel register
        and Enter confirms. Same fix applied to the asset Rename and Save-as-Prefab
        dialogs (they were re-opening every frame, eating the button clicks).
      * Ctrl+Z now undoes scene-view edits: moving/rotating/scaling with the gizmo
        and moving/resizing UI widgets each record an undo step. Ctrl+Shift+Z redo
        no longer cancels itself out.
      * 3D scene view: clicking empty space (or a UI element) now deselects the
        selected 3D object, matching the 2D view.
  - UI / Text:
      * New UI widgets spawn centered on screen instead of in the top-left corner.
      * Text (UI) is reworked around a proper bounding box: it's now selectable and
        resizable in the Scene view, no longer "glitchy" (stable rect, readable
        default size), supports multi-line text, horizontal align + vertical
        centering, and an optional background box. New Text starts at a sensible
        size, centered.

v2.6.0
  - More scripting:
      * Mathf: Clamp01, InverseLerp, Repeat, DeltaAngle, LerpAngle,
        Approximately (and plain clamp01 / inverse_lerp / delta_angle /
        lerp_angle / approximately), plus Mathf.Log/Exp/Asin/Acos/Atan.
      * Color.Lerp(a, b, t) (lerp_color) to blend colors for fades/flashes.
      * C#-style .Length / .Count on string and array variables
        (name.Length, items.Count).
      * Vector3.Angle / Vector2.Angle (vec_angle), Vector2.Lerp /
        Vector3.LerpUnclamped, and Input.GetButton/GetButtonDown/GetButtonUp.
    Backward compatible.

v2.5.0
  - More scripting:
      * Color values — Color.red/green/blue/white/black/yellow/cyan/magenta/gray
        and Color(r,g,b)/new Color(r,g,b); pass them to set_color / set_tint
        (which still accept separate r,g,b[,a] numbers too).
      * String formatting — format("HP {0}/{1}", a, b) positional and
        format("HP {} / {}", a, b) sequential (also string.Format), plus
        pad_left / pad_right.
    Backward compatible.

v2.4.0
  - More scripting, vector-focused:
      * Vector3 properties: v.magnitude, v.sqrMagnitude, v.normalized (plus the
        existing v.x/v.y/v.z), and vec_move_towards / Vector3.MoveTowards.
      * Direction & input vectors: transform.forward / up / right and
        Input.mousePosition return Vector3 values.
      * Mathf.PerlinNoise(x, y) — smooth 2D noise in [0,1].
    Fully backward compatible.

v2.3.0
  - Even more scripting:
      * Vector3 values you can actually work with: var v = new Vector3(3,4,0);
        read/write v.x / v.y / v.z directly, plus vec_add / vec_sub / vec_scale /
        vec_length / vec_dot / vec_distance / vec_normalize / vec_lerp / vec_x/y/z
        (and Vector3.Lerp / Dot / Normalize / Distance).
      * do { ... } while (cond); loops (run the body at least once).
    Fully backward compatible.

v2.2.0
  - More scripting power, still Unity-flavored:
      * String interpolation: $"Score: {score}  next={score + 1}" — embed any
        expression in { } (use {{ }} for literal braces).
      * switch (x) { case 1: ...; break; default: ...; } — C-style fall-through
        until break; case labels can be numbers or strings.
      * More Mathf: Clamp01, Repeat, InverseLerp, Approximately, DeltaAngle,
        LerpAngle.
    Fully backward compatible.

v2.1.0
  - Easier-to-read scripting: ~90 plain-English aliases so you can usually guess
    the right word — place_at / move_by / position_x / set_position_x / turn /
    face, key_pressed / is_key_held / input_horizontal, create_object /
    remove_object / show_object / hide_object / object_exists, call_later /
    repeat_every / time_elapsed, set_tint / set_sprite / set_background_color /
    emit_particles / play_sfx, apply_force / apply_impulse / is_overlapping /
    cast_ray, set_camera / set_zoom, save_value / load_number / load_text,
    random_float / random_chance, log, and more. Each maps onto an existing
    builtin, so old scripts and the Unity-style names all keep working. New
    "Plain-English names" table in docs/scripting.md.

v2.0.0
  - Unity-style scripting, completed. Building on v1.99, OkayScript now also
    accepts:
      * foreach (var item in list) { ... }  (C# foreach).
      * Generic calls: GetComponent<Rigidbody2D>() (truthy if present) and
        AddComponent<Rigidbody2D>() (adds SpriteRenderer / Rigidbody2D /
        TextRenderer / BoxCollider2D at runtime).
      * Quaternion.Euler(x, y, z) / Quaternion.identity, assignable to
        transform.rotation.
      * C# attributes like [SerializeField] and [Header("Stats")] are accepted
        (ignored) before fields and methods.
    The editor's "new script" template is now a Unity-style MonoBehaviour, and a
    real Unity script frequently pastes in unchanged. Still fully backward
    compatible with the classic function start()/update(dt) style.

v1.99.0
  - Scripting now reads like Unity C#. You can write OkayScript almost exactly
    like a Unity MonoBehaviour, and a real Unity script often pastes in unchanged:
      * Lifecycle: void Start() / void Update() / Awake() / LateUpdate() (the
        classic start/update still work), plus a "public class Foo : MonoBehaviour
        { ... }" wrapper whose methods & fields are hoisted out automatically.
      * Dotted properties: transform.position(.x/.y/.z), transform.localScale,
        transform.eulerAngles.z, Time.deltaTime/time/timeScale, gameObject.name/
        activeSelf/tag, Screen.width/height, Input.mousePosition, Mathf.PI,
        Random.value, Vector3.zero/one/up/... — readable AND assignable
        (transform.position = new Vector3(x, y, z); transform.position.x = 5;).
      * Dotted methods: Input.GetKey/GetKeyDown/GetAxis/GetMouseButton...,
        Mathf.Sin/Cos/Sqrt/Clamp/Lerp/..., Debug.Log, transform.Translate/Rotate,
        gameObject.SetActive, Instantiate/Destroy, Physics2D.Raycast,
        Random.Range, SceneManager.LoadScene.
      * Language: typed declarations (int x = 0; float speed = 5f;), f/d numeric
        suffixes, i++ / ++i / i--, and new Vector3()/Vector2() constructors.
      * Unity event names: OnCollisionEnter / OnTriggerEnter / OnClick /
        OnValueChanged (alongside the classic on_collision etc).
    Fully backward compatible — existing scripts are untouched.

v1.98.0
  - DOTween for UI + more transform tweens: tween_ui_move / tween_ui_size animate
    a UI widget's anchored position and size (slide menus in, pop panels open) —
    UI widgets don't use the Transform, so these target the widget directly. Also
    tween_rotate_to (rotate to an ABSOLUTE angle via shortest path, vs the
    relative tween_rotate) and tween_scale_xy (non-uniform scale). And tween_color
    / tween_fade now also drive UI Image and UI Panel colors, not just sprites and
    meshes.

v1.97.0
  - DOTween, even bigger: tween_move_by (relative glide), tween_jump (arc-jump
    to a target peaking at a height — coins flying to the HUD, hops), tween_path
    (move through a list of waypoints), tween_loop_scale (pulsing) and
    tween_loop_rotate (continuous spin — loaders, coins), and tween_number
    (count a sibling text number up/down with an optional prefix — score ticks,
    damage popups). Joins the on-complete callbacks, ping-pong, punch and shake
    added last update.

v1.96.0
  - Raycasting from script: new raycast(ox,oy,dx,dy[,max]) returns the NAME of
    the object hit ("" = miss), with full details via ray_hit(), ray_object(),
    ray_x()/ray_y() (hit point), ray_nx()/ray_ny() (surface normal) and
    ray_dist(). 3D mirror: raycast3(ox,oy,oz,dx,dy,dz[,max]) + ray3_*(). Great
    for shooting, line-of-sight, ground checks, and click-to-select. (The old
    boolean raycast_hit()/raycast_hit3() still work.)
  - DOTween, expanded: every tween (tween_move/move3/scale/rotate/color/fade)
    now takes an optional on-complete callback (a function name) as the last
    argument. New tween_loop_move(x,y,dur[,ease]) ping-pongs forever (floaters,
    patrols), tween_punch_scale(amount,dur) / tween_punch_pos(dx,dy,dur) add
    "juice" to buttons & pickups, and tween_shake(intensity,dur) does impact /
    camera shake — all settle cleanly back to where they started.
  - Drag & drop, expanded: world Draggables gain Grid Snap (snap to a world grid
    for board / tile games) and Drag Scale (lift the item visually while
    dragging). Drop Zones now fire on_hover_enter() / on_hover_exit() on a
    dragged item entering/leaving them, so slots can react during the drag.

v1.95.0
  - Drag & drop for world items (not just UI): add a "Draggable (item)" component
    to a sprite to pick it up and move it around the scene at runtime, and a
    "Drop Zone (item)" to sprites that can receive it. Dropping fires on_drop()
    on the item and on_receive() on the zone (scripts read drop_source() &
    drop_target()). Same rich options as the UI version — lock axis, drag
    threshold, bring-to-front, snap-onto-zone (instant world-space slots),
    return-to-start, and an accept-tag filter. Zones highlight while a valid item
    hovers them. Great for board games, crafting, card games, and loot.

v1.94.0
  - Drop-target feedback: UI Drop Target now highlights itself while a valid
    draggable hovers over it during a drag (toggle "Highlight on hover" and pick
    the overlay color in the inspector). Makes inventory slots feel responsive —
    you can see exactly where an item will land before you let go.

v1.93.0
  - UI drag & drop, much more customizable: UI Draggable now has a "Lock axis"
    option (Both/Horizontal/Vertical) for sliders & rails, a "Drag threshold"
    (pixels the pointer must move before a drag starts, so a click stays a
    click), and "Bring to front" to draw the item above its siblings while
    dragging. UI Drop Target gains an "Accept tag" filter so a slot only takes
    matching items (e.g. a weapon slot that accepts only "weapon" items). All
    exposed in the inspector and saved with the scene.

v1.92.0
  - Scriptable Objects (Data Assets): reusable data files (.okaydata) of named
    fields for item/enemy/level definitions and config — Unity's ScriptableObject
    idea. Create via Project > New Data Asset and edit fields in a popup; read
    from OkayScript with data_num/data_str/data_has, and customize from code with
    data_set + data_save. Resolved beside the game in built builds.

v1.91.0
  - Inventory made trivial: UI Draggable gets "Snap into slot" — drop an item on
    a slot and it centers itself there, no scripting required. New Project >
    "Inventory (drag & drop)" template stamps out a bag with slots + draggable
    items you can rearrange immediately.

v1.90.0
  - UI drag & drop: add a "UI Draggable" component to a widget to drag it at
    runtime; add "UI Drop Target" to widgets that can receive it. Dropping fires
    on_drop() on the dragged object and on_receive() on the target (the names are
    in Prefs / scripts read ui_drop_source() & ui_drop_target()). Optional
    return-to-start. The building blocks for inventories, card games, etc.
  - Mouse-over-UI from script: ui_pointer_over(), ui_hovered() (name under the
    cursor), ui_is_hovered("name").

v1.89.0
  - Mouse wheel now reaches the game: a new Input wheel channel (MouseWheel /
    FeedMouseWheel) fed by the player and editor. Scroll Views now actually
    wheel-scroll in built games (and in editor Play), not just in edit-mode
    authoring — fixing scrollable lists/menus in shipped games.

v1.88.0
  - Button icons: a UI Button can show an image (PNG/JPG) left of its label —
    set Icon + Icon Size in the inspector (or icon=/iconsize= in OkayUI markup).
    The label shifts to make room, the icon is bundled by Build Game, and it
    renders in the built game (the editor shows a placeholder box).

v1.87.0
  - Dropdown placeholder: set a placeholder string and a value of -1 ("Clear
    selection" in the inspector) to show dimmed prompt text until the player
    picks an option. Authorable in OkayUI markup (placeholder="..."); saves with
    the scene and renders in the editor and built game.

v1.86.0
  - UI Slider can be Vertical: drag bottom-to-top and the fill/knob run
    vertically — for volume columns, mixers and meters. Toggle "Vertical" in the
    inspector; saves with the scene and works in the editor and built game.

v1.85.0
  - Interactable (disabled) state for UI Toggle, Slider and Dropdown (Button
    already had it): toggle it off to grey the widget out and have it ignore
    input — for settings that depend on others. Saves with the scene; the
    disabled look renders in the editor and built game.

v1.84.0
  - Text gets a Bold option (faux-bold by double-striking the glyphs) for
    headings and emphasis — in the Inspector, OkayUI markup (bold=1), saved with
    the scene, and rendered in the editor and built game.

v1.83.0
  - UI Toggle gains a Style: classic Checkbox or a modern on/off Switch (a pill
    track with a sliding knob, with its own knob color) — pick it in the
    inspector. Saves with the scene and renders in editor + built game.

v1.82.0
  - More UI polish settings: UI Button gets a "Hover Grow" (scales up when
    hovered/focused — tactile menus), and UI Panel gets a "Drop Shadow"
    (color + offset) to lift dialogs and cards off the scene. Both save with
    the scene and render in the editor and built game.

v1.81.0
  - More widget settings: UI Slider gains "Whole Numbers" (snaps the value to
    integers — great for difficulty/count pickers), and UI Progress Bar gains a
    Fill Direction (Left→Right, Right→Left, Bottom→Top, Top→Bottom) for vertical
    bars and right-anchored meters. Both save with the scene and render in the
    built game.

v1.80.0
  - Full controller/keyboard menu navigation: focus now moves across Buttons,
    Toggles, Sliders and Dropdowns (not just buttons). Up/Down change focus;
    Enter/Space/A activate a button, flip a toggle, or open a dropdown; Left/
    Right change a dropdown selection or nudge a slider by 5%. The focused
    widget shows a highlight ring in the built game.

v1.79.0
  - The Main Menu project template now showcases the modern UI: a rounded,
    gradient card with an outlined title, a styled Start button (with tooltip),
    a volume Slider with a live value bound to a pref, a Fullscreen toggle, a
    Quality dropdown, and a Quit button — a ready-made settings menu to learn
    from or build on.

v1.78.0
  - UI Toolkit: input fields in OkayUI markup support type=integer|decimal|
    password (e.g. `input type=password placeholder="PIN"`), matching the
    Input Field content types.

v1.77.0
  - Snapping now applies while resizing too: dragging a resize handle snaps the
    grabbed edge to the canvas edges/center and to sibling widgets (with the
    magenta guide), falling back to the pixel grid on axes that don't lock on.

v1.76.0
  - Stretch helpers: a selected resizable UI widget's inspector gains Fill Width
    / Fill Height / Fill Canvas buttons that size it to the canvas on that axis
    and align it to the edge (respecting its anchor) — quick full-bleed panels
    and bars.

v1.75.0
  - Anchor presets (Unity-style 3x3 grid) in a selected UI widget's inspector:
    click a corner/edge/center to set the anchor. The widget keeps its on-screen
    position — its pixel offset is recomputed for the new anchor — so switching
    anchors never makes it jump. The current anchor is highlighted.

v1.74.0
  - UI layering + centering: a selected UI widget's inspector gains Bring to
    Front / Send to Back (controls draw order and which overlapping widget gets
    picked) and Center in Canvas (centers the widget for its anchor). Backed by
    new Scene::MoveToFront/MoveToBack.

v1.73.0
  - Arrow-key nudging: with a UI widget selected and the Scene focused, the
    arrow keys move it one pixel at a time (hold Shift to step by the UI snap
    grid) for precise placement. Keys repeat while held.

v1.72.0
  - UI snapping (Unity-style): with Snap on, dragging/resizing a UI widget snaps
    to a pixel grid (set the "UI px" size in the Scene toolbar, default 8), and
    moving smart-snaps the widget's edges/center to the canvas edges/center and
    to other widgets — drawing magenta alignment guides where it locks on.

v1.71.0
  - Input Field overhaul: typing now comes from the OS text-input event, so
    uppercase, punctuation and symbols all work (Shift / Caps respected) instead
    of the old lowercase-only guesswork. A blinking caret, horizontal scrolling
    (the caret stays visible in long text), Esc-to-cancel, and content types
    (Standard / Integer / Decimal / Password, with a mask char) round it out.
    Works the same in the editor and built games.

v1.70.0
  - Everything displays in builds: the standalone player now renders UI Input
    Fields (box, text/placeholder, focus outline, caret) and Scroll Views
    (background + scrollbar) — previously these showed only in the editor.
  - Scroll View contents now scroll and clip correctly in built games: child
    widgets are offset by the scroll position and clipped to the viewport, so
    lists hide off-view rows just like in the editor preview.
  - Verified every UI widget type (panel, text, button, image, slider, toggle,
    progress, input, dropdown, scroll view, tooltip) renders in the build;
    Layout Group arranges its children on start.

v1.69.0
  - Better UI resizing: handles now resize in screen space, so the grabbed edge
    follows the cursor correctly for ANY anchor (previously right/center/bottom-
    anchored widgets drifted because changing size shifts the anchor). The
    opposite edge stays pinned, with an 8px minimum; handles are easier to grab.
  - Snap (toolbar) now rounds UI moves/resizes to whole pixels for tidy values.
  - Anchoring is visible: a selected widget shows a marker at the canvas point
    it's anchored to with a line to the widget, plus a live "W x H" size readout
    above the selection box.

v1.68.0
  - Fix: the newer UI widgets (Scroll View and Layout Group) couldn't be
    selected or resized in the Scene view because they weren't recognized as
    UI elements. Scroll View is now fully selectable/resizable; the Layout Group
    is selectable and movable by its origin (it's a controller, so no resize).
  - Added a test that creates one of every UI widget and verifies all are
    scene-selectable and survive save/load.

v1.67.0
  - Fix: selecting and resizing screen-space UI in the Scene view was
    unreliable — the canvas "hovered" test could read false in some docking /
    overlap states (and while mid-drag), so clicks on a widget did nothing.
    Hover detection is now robust (window-hover + pointer-in-canvas fallback,
    and stays true while the canvas is the active drag item).
  - A selected UI widget no longer shows the world move/rotate/scale gizmo
    (only its UI selection box + resize handles), so the handles are clear.

v1.66.0
  - UI Toolkit parameterized custom widgets: a `define` body can use $args, and
    each instance supplies them — define a `card` once with text "$title", then
    `card title="Hello"` / `card title="World"` stamp out copies with their own
    text. Longest-name-first substitution; works in labels and any value.

v1.65.0
  - Data binding now works on buttons and input fields too (not just text):
    `button bind="Coins: {coins}"` keeps the label live. UITextBind updates
    whichever text-bearing widget it sits on (text / button / input).

v1.64.0
  - UI Toolkit percent sizing: any pos/size component can be a percentage of the
    canvas — e.g. `panel pos=10%,25% size=50%,40` is positioned a tenth in,
    a quarter down, half the canvas wide and 40px tall. Mix percent and pixels
    freely; resolved against the canvas when the document builds.

v1.63.0
  - UI Toolkit live validation: the UI Document now reports markup problems —
    unknown widget types and unrecognized keys (typos) — each with its line
    number. The inspector shows "No problems." when clean, or a red list to fix.
    Available in code via UIDocument::Diagnostics().

v1.62.0
  - UI data binding: a text widget can bind to live values —
    `text bind="Score: {score}"` shows the current `score` Prefs value and
    updates every frame as it changes (set it from script with prefs_set).
    Multiple {keys} per label; {{ }} emit literal braces. Backed by a new
    UITextBind component that also serializes for hand-placed text.

v1.61.0
  - UI Toolkit containers: author `scroll` (clipped, scrollable region with
    content= height) and `layout` (dir=vertical|horizontal, spacing=, padding=)
    directly in OkayUI markup — parent widgets to them for scrollable, auto-
    arranged lists with no hand-placing.
  - name=<id> on any markup widget sets its GameObject name, so the ui_* script
    API (ui_set_text, ui_slider_value, …) can target widgets the document built.
  - active=0|1 starts a widget shown/hidden.

v1.60.0
  - UI Toolkit (OkayUI markup) greatly expanded: the UI Document now authors the
    full widget set — added `input` and `dropdown` to panel/text/button/image/
    slider/toggle/progress — plus a `tooltip="..."` key on ANY widget.
  - New customization keys in markup: corner=, border=/bordercolor=, gradient=,
    font=, hover=/pressed=/textcolor=, align=, outline=, shadow=, fill=/amount=,
    showvalue=, percent=, check=, options=A|B|C, min=/max=.
  - New event handlers: onchange= (slider/dropdown), ontoggle= (toggle),
    onsubmit= (input) — alongside onclick=. Handlers take the rest of the line.
  - Inspector: an "Insert Example" button drops in a styled settings menu so you
    can see the toolkit in action immediately.

v1.59.0
  - UI Tooltips (GameObject > UI > Tooltip): add a UI Tooltip to any widget and
    a hint box appears next to the cursor after hovering it for a short delay.
    Tunable text, delay, background and text color; saves with the scene and
    shows in the built game (and the editor's Game view while playing).

v1.58.0
  - Slider/Progress/Toggle customization: rounded corners on all three; the
    Slider gets an adjustable Knob Size and an optional value readout; the
    Progress Bar gets an optional centered percent readout (with text color).
  - Consistent "tons of options" styling across the widget set; everything
    saves with the scene and renders in the built game.

v1.57.0
  - UI Image fill (like Unity's Filled image): set Fill Mode to Left/Right/Up/
    Down and a Fill Amount (0..1) to reveal only part of the image — perfect for
    cooldown sweeps, health/mana bars and loading fills. Drive it from script
    with ui_set_fill("name", v). Works on textured images (reveals a slice) and
    on plain colored rects.
  - UI Image rounded corners (Corner Radius) for the colored-rect fallback.
  - All of it saves with the scene and renders in the built game.

v1.56.0
  - UI scripting API: drive any widget from OkayScript by name —
    ui_set_text / ui_get_text, ui_clicked, ui_set_interactable,
    ui_slider_value / ui_set_slider, ui_toggle_value / ui_set_toggle,
    ui_dropdown_value / ui_dropdown_text / ui_set_dropdown, and
    ui_set_progress. One controller script can now wire up a whole menu
    (read a slider, flip a toggle, pick a dropdown option, update a label).
    Documented in docs/SCRIPTING.md.

v1.55.0
  - Panel gradients: a UI Panel can fade top-to-bottom — turn on "Gradient" and
    set a Bottom Color (Color is the top). Great for headers and backdrops.
  - Text outline: screen-space and world Text gets an "Outline" option (a
    1-pixel surround in an Outline Color) so labels read on any background —
    stacks with the existing drop shadow.
  - Both save with the scene and render the same in the built game.

v1.54.0
  - UI Dropdown (GameObject > UI > Dropdown): an option selector like Unity's
    Dropdown. Click to open the list, pick an option to set the value and call
    the script's on_change(). Edit the option list, selection, and colors
    (header / highlight / list / text / border) in the Inspector; everything
    saves with the scene and renders in the built game.

v1.53.0
  - UI customization: panels and buttons now have a Corner Radius, an optional
    Border (width + color), and buttons have a Font Scale for the label size.
    Edit them in the Inspector; all of it saves with the scene and renders in
    the built game.
  - Text alignment: screen-space Text has a Left/Center/Right Align option,
    measured from its anchored Screen Pos.

v1.52.0
  - UI Input Field (GameObject > UI > Input Field): click to focus and type;
    Backspace deletes, Enter calls on_submit(). Placeholder + max length; saves.
  - More tweens: tween_rotate (spin about Z), tween_color and tween_fade
    (animate a sprite/mesh albedo or opacity), all with easings.

v1.51.0
  - UI Scroll View: a wheel-scrollable, clipped region (GameObject > UI > Scroll
    View). Parent widgets to it; set Content Height; the mouse wheel scrolls and
    off-view children are hidden, with a scrollbar.
  - UI Layout Group: vertical/horizontal auto-layout (GameObject > UI > Layout
    Group) that stacks child widgets by size + spacing — scroll view + layout =
    scrollable lists with no hand-placing.
  - Tweening (DOTween-style): tween_move / tween_move3 / tween_scale with easings
    (out_quad, in_out_sine, out_back, out_bounce, ...).
  - Save system: save_game / load_game / save_exists / delete_save snapshot the
    scene to named slots.

v1.50.0
  - Reliable networking: net_send_reliable / net_send_reliable_to resend until
    the other end acks and de-dupe on arrival — for events you can't drop
    (deaths, score, "go!"). Works client->server and server->all/one.
  - Moderation: net_kick(id, "reason") disconnects a client; the client learns it
    via net_was_kicked() (and KickReason). Also a net_kick ActionList op.
  - Steam: ownership + DLC (steam_owns, steam_owns_dlc), achievement enumeration
    (steam_achievement_count) and steam_language(). The simulation grants
    ownership so those paths are testable.

v1.49.0
  - Unity-style Console: severity icons + colors (info/warning/error), a
    Collapse toggle (groups repeats with a count), per-level filter buttons with
    live counts, a search box, Clear-on-Play, timestamps, and a details pane for
    the selected entry. Script/engine logs route in with the right severity.
  - Easier hosting + more host options: the Network Manager and the Services
    panel gain Server Name, Password, Max Players and Tick Rate. The server now
    refuses joins when full or on a wrong password (clients see "join refused"),
    and clients learn the server's name. All settings save with the scene.

v1.48.0
  - Script debugging in the editor Console: engine + script logs now stream into
    the Console. New builtins print, debug_log, log_info, log_warn, log_error,
    trace, watch("name", v), assert(cond, "msg"), and format("{} of {}", a, b).
  - Easier coding — lots more helpers: approach, remap, frac, mod, snap, is_nan,
    is_finite, avg, min3/max3, lerp_angle, concat, str_repeat.
  - Proper/intuitive function names (aliases): delta_time, get_key(_down/_up),
    random/random_int, pick, distance, instantiate, destroy_self, translate,
    set_position, play_audio, to_string/str, to_number/num, get_x/y/z,
    screen_width/height.

v1.47.0
  - Terrain (like Unity): a heightmap terrain you sculpt with a brush — drag in
    the 3D Scene view to raise, hold Shift to lower (tunable radius/strength) —
    or generate with Flatten / Smooth / Randomize / Hills. Create via GameObject
    > 3D Object > Terrain; the heightmap renders as a mesh and saves with scene.
  - Materials: reusable surface presets (albedo, emissive, specular, texture,
    tiling, unlit, double-sided) saved as .okaymat assets and applied to any
    Mesh Renderer — Save/Load in the inspector, or drop a .okaymat from Project.

v1.46.0
  - Lobby ready-up + start-match (built on rooms): clients call net_ready(1);
    the host polls net_ready_count() / net_all_ready() and calls net_start_match()
    when everyone's set — every peer in that room then reports net_match_started().
    Scoped per room, so matches are independent. Also no-code ActionList ops
    (net_ready, net_start_match).

v1.45.0
  - Lobbies / rooms: peers in different rooms are fully isolated — they only see
    avatars and receive broadcast messages from others in the same room, so one
    server can host many independent matches. Set it with net_room("name")
    before host/join, or the "Room (lobby)" field on the Network Manager.
  - Steam leaderboard reads: steam_leaderboard_top("board", n) returns the
    ranked Top-N as an array of "rank,name,score" strings.

v1.44.0
  - Smoother multiplayer: remote avatars are now interpolated toward their latest
    networked position instead of snapping (tunable "Smoothing" on the Network
    Manager; first sample still snaps so nothing slides in from the origin).
  - Latency readout: clients ping the server ~1x/sec; net_ping() returns the
    round-trip in ms, shown in the Network Manager inspector + Services panel.

v1.43.0
  - Replicated spawning: net_spawn("prefab", x, y[, z]) instantiates a prefab on
    every peer with one call (bullets, pickups, effects) — the server relays it
    so all clients make the same object. Also a no-code ActionList op (net_spawn).
  - More Steam from scripts: steam_progress (achievement progress), steam_presence
    (rich presence), steam_friends, steam_overlay. Docs updated.

v1.42.0
  - No-code multiplayer: a Network Manager component with Auto Start = Host/Join
    on Play (port, host IP, player name) in the Inspector — add it, pick a mode,
    press Play. It defaults its own object as the broadcast avatar and mirrors
    peers as sprites; settings save with the scene.
  - Synced variables: net_set(key, value) / net_get(key) — a server-authoritative
    shared store every peer sees, auto-synced to new joiners. Also a no-code
    ActionList op (net_set).
  - Steam from scripts: a process-wide shared Steam service plus steam_* builtins
    (name, unlock/is_unlocked/clear, set/get/inc stat, store, leaderboard,
    cloud read/write). No-code ActionList ops too (steam_unlock, steam_set_stat,
    steam_inc_stat). Docs updated.

v1.41.0
  - Better performance: backface culling (solid meshes skip triangles facing
    away — about half the triangles of a closed mesh, for free) and a per-mesh
    frustum cull (off-screen meshes are dropped before any per-triangle work).
    Both apply in the editor's 3D view and the shipped player.
  - Better graphics: optional distance fog (per scene) that fades far geometry
    toward a fog color between a start/end distance — a depth cue that also
    hides far-clip pop-in. Toggle + tune under Stats > Environment; it saves
    with the scene and renders in the built game.

v1.40.0
  - Multiplayer made easy: a full net_* OkayScript API — net_host(port) /
    net_join(ip, port) let any player host or join their own server, plus
    net_send / net_send_to / net_poll (+ net_msg_channel/data/from),
    net_is_server / net_id / net_peers / net_name. The same actions are
    no-code ActionList ops (net_host, net_join, net_send, net_disconnect).
  - New Project > Multiplayer template: press H to host, J to join, WASD to
    move — a complete runnable example.
  - Updated README + docs with a "Multiplayer in ~5 lines" guide, the net and
    Scene Manager script tables, and an ActionList op reference.

v1.39.0
  - Unity-like Build Settings: the Build dialog now has product/company names,
    window width/height (with presets), fullscreen / resizable / vsync, "include
    all project scenes", and a development-build flag. Builds write a
    game.okayconfig the player reads for the window + scene list, and can bundle
    every project scene (so load_scene_index works in the shipped game).

v1.38.0
  - Added a placeholder OkaySpace logo (a ringed planet over a starfield), drawn
    procedurally and set as the window icon for both the editor and the launcher.

v1.37.0
  - Expanded scripting: SceneManager from OkayScript — load_scene_index(i),
    load_scene_name(n), load_next_scene(), reload_scene(), scene_count(),
    scene_index(), scene_name().
  - Expanded visual scripting (ActionList): new instructions mul_var, div_var,
    copy_var, rand_var, set_prefs, add_prefs, save_prefs, set_tag,
    load_scene_index, load_next_scene; new conditions var_neq, key_up,
    mouse_down, prefs_eq, prefs_gt. All selectable in the visual editor.

v1.36.0
  - New Scene Manager (engine + a Scenes panel): a build list of the project's
    scenes with by-index / by-name loading, Load Next and Reload — the engine
    analogue of Unity's SceneManager + Build Settings. The Scenes panel lists
    and opens project scenes and feeds the build order.
  - Prefabs from the Hierarchy: right-click an object > Save As Prefab... writes
    a .okayprefab into the project (drag it back in to instantiate).
  - Expanded Hierarchy right-click: Copy / Paste As Sibling / Paste As Child,
    Duplicate, Create Empty Child, Create Child (Cube/Sprite/Camera/Light),
    Focus, Rename, Activate, Delete; the empty-space menu adds Camera, Light,
    a 3D Object submenu and Paste.

v1.35.0
  - Build everything with the skybox and lighting: the sky gradient and a base
    ambient level are now per-scene render settings saved with the scene, so a
    built game (the player) renders the same sky + lighting the editor shows —
    not just an editor preview. Edit them under Stats > Environment (Sky &
    Light); the menu Skybox toggle is now per-scene too.

v1.34.0
  - Fixed the updater "doesn't always update": the GitHub raw CDN was serving a
    cached VERSION.txt / .exe. Every download now sends no-cache headers and a
    unique query string so you always get the freshly published build (editor
    self-update and the launcher both).
  - Removed PlayFab. Online services are now Steam + Multiplayer.
  - Expanded the Steam SDK surface: stat increment, achievement progress
    (auto-unlocks at the max), leaderboards (submit + ranked Top-N), Steam
    Cloud (write/read/has/delete), friend count and overlay. The Services panel
    exposes all of it; the simulation backend implements it in memory.
  - Expanded multiplayer: player names in the join handshake, a server-side
    roster (Peers()/PeerName()), peer joined/left callbacks, and SendTo(id, ..)
    targeted messages (private/RPC) alongside the existing broadcast. The
    Multiplayer panel gains a name field, roster and a simple chat box.

v1.33.0
  - Scene view: resize UI as well as move it. A selected widget shows 8 grab
    handles — drag a corner/edge to resize (respecting the Canvas scale), drag
    the body to move. Works in the 2D and 3D Scene views.
  - UIDocument is now a real little UI toolkit: reusable styles and custom
    widgets. `style name color=.. size=..` defines a USS-style class a widget
    pulls in with `class=name` (its own keys win); `define name` + an indented
    block defines a custom control that `name pos=x,y` instances and shifts.

v1.32.0
  - UI now works the Unity way: widgets created from GameObject > UI are
    automatically parented under a Canvas (created if missing) and the scene
    gets one Event System. Each Canvas scales its child widgets to the window
    (Constant Pixel Size or Scale With Screen Size), so a HUD designed at one
    resolution adapts to others. Click-to-select and drag respect that scale.
  - New "UI Toolkit"-style UIDocument: author a whole menu/HUD as OkayUI markup
    (one widget per line, indent to nest) and the engine builds the widget tree
    for you. Edit the markup + Rebuild live from the Inspector. Types: panel,
    text, button, image, slider, toggle, progress; keys: pos, size, color,
    anchor, value, on, onclick. Add via GameObject > UI > UI Document.

v1.31.0
  - Scene view: click any UI widget to select it, then drag to reposition it —
    works in BOTH the 2D and 3D Scene views (UI used to only be selectable in
    2D). World picking yields to UI so a HUD click selects the HUD.
  - New Canvas component (Unity-style CanvasScaler): Constant Pixel Size or
    Scale With Screen Size with a reference resolution and width/height match.
  - New Event System component: central UI pointer dispatch — tracks the
    hovered / pressed / selected widget and exposes IsPointerOverUI(). Added
    UIRaycast() and a uniform UIRect view of every widget.
  - New 3D Platformer starter template: perspective camera, directional light,
    a physics ground and a Character Controller 3D player (New Project > 3D
    Platformer).
  - Networking: a custom message channel (RPC / events / chat). Send(channel,
    data) fans out to every peer; receive via a handler or TakeMessages().
    The server relays client messages to all other peers.

v1.30.0
  - New Follow Target 2D component: no-code chase AI — moves toward a named
    object at a set speed and stops within a distance (enemies, homing).

v1.29.0
  - Inspector: drag an asset from the Project onto a Texture or WAV field to
    set it (Mesh Renderer texture, Sprite texture, Audio Source clip).

v1.28.0
  - Hierarchy: double-click a row (or right-click > Rename) to rename it, and
    Activate/Deactivate from the right-click menu.

v1.27.0
  - Hierarchy: drag a row onto another to re-parent it; drop it on empty space
    to unparent. Drag a prefab/scene from the Project into the Hierarchy to
    instantiate / open it.
  - Fixed: 'Show in Explorer' now opens the OS file manager instead of VS Code.

v1.26.0
  - Project drag & drop: drag an asset onto a folder (in the grid or the tree)
    to move it there.
  - New Character Controller 3D: no-code WASD movement on the ground plane with
    jump; drives a Rigidbody3D if present.

v1.25.0
  - New Character Controller 2D component: no-code Top-Down or Platformer
    movement (WASD/arrows, jump). Drives a Rigidbody2D if present.
  - Removed the old node-graph Visual Script from the editor — the no-code
    Actions system replaces it (old scenes still load).

v1.24.0
  - Visual scripting: reorder conditions and instructions with the ^ / v
    buttons on each row.

v1.23.0
  - Signals (Game Creator-style): an Actions list can use the On Message
    trigger and the `send` instruction to broadcast/react to named events.
    OkayScript can broadcast too via send_message("name"), so scripts and
    visual scripts talk to each other.

v1.22.0
  - Inspector Lock: pin the inspector to one object so it stays put while you
    click around the scene/hierarchy.
  - Toolbar tooltips on Play / Stop / Pause / Step / Build.

v1.21.0
  - Project tab shows real image thumbnails for PNG/JPG/etc. assets.
  - Right-click empty space in Project for New Folder / New Script / Show in
    Explorer.

v1.20.0
  - Scripting Reference now teaches OkayScript from scratch: a "Learn the basics"
    tutorial (variables, math, if/else, loops, start()/update(dt), functions)
    with runnable examples — open it from Help > Scripting Reference.
  - Visual scripting (Actions) expanded: On Key Up trigger; goto / stop for loops
    and early-out; spawn3 (3D), set_timescale, and more condition ops (key_down,
    mouse, has_tag, is_active, dist_gt, exists).
  - OkayScript expanded: set_emissive / set_unlit / set_specular / set_tiling /
    set_mesh_texture, set_glyph / sprite_size, call("fn") for dynamic dispatch,
    and gc_get / gc_set to share variables with the Actions visual scripting.
  - Inspector: Reset Pos / Rot / Scale buttons on Transform.
  - Project: right-click > Show in Explorer.

v1.19.0
  - No-code visual scripting (Game Creator-style): a new "Actions" component
    with a Trigger (On Start / Update / Key / Collision / Click), a list of
    Conditions (key, chance, var compare, distance, tag, exists…), and a list of
    Instructions that run top-to-bottom (move, rotate, scale, wait, set/add var,
    set color/text, velocity/impulse, emit, play sound/anim, camera/light, spawn,
    destroy, load scene, log…). Authored from dropdowns in the inspector; runs in
    the editor and the player; saved with the scene.
  - Expanded OkayScript: set_emissive / set_unlit / set_specular / set_tiling /
    set_mesh_texture, and gc_get / gc_set to share variables with the Actions
    visual scripting.

v1.18.0
  - 3D positional audio: an Audio Source can be marked 3D (spatial) with Min/Max
    Distance, and the mixer attenuates it by distance to the listener (the main
    camera). Great for world sounds that fade as you move away.

v1.17.0
  - Unity-style Project tab: a folder tree on the left, an icon grid on the
    right with type-colored thumbnails, a clickable breadcrumb, and search.
    Double-click to enter folders / open scenes / instantiate prefabs / open
    scripts. New Folder + New Script buttons, plus right-click Rename / Delete.
  - Fly the Scene camera: hold the right mouse button and use WASD to move
    (Q/E down/up, Shift to move faster) while you look around.
  - Fixed models glitching/vanishing when you zoom in very close — the renderer
    now clips triangles against the camera near plane instead of dropping them.
  - More UI polish: refined dark theme (spacing, scrollbars, contrast, rounding).

v1.16.0
  - Proper UV wrapping on Sphere and Cylinder primitives, so textures wrap
    around them cleanly instead of using the planar decal fallback.
  - Texture tiling: a per-object Tiling (U, V) control repeats the texture
    across the surface (Mesh Renderer inspector; saved with the scene).

v1.15.0
  - Textured 3D meshes: set a Texture image on a Mesh Renderer and it's mapped
    onto the mesh (planar/box projection — exact on cube/plane/quad faces),
    tinted by Color and lit by the material. Works in the editor and the
    standalone player; Build Game bundles the image. Textures are cached.

v1.14.0
  - Materials on 3D objects: the Mesh Renderer now has Emissive (self-glow),
    Specular + Shininess (Blinn-Phong highlights), and an Unlit toggle, all
    honored by the solid renderer in both the editor and the player. Tune them
    in the inspector; they save with the scene.

v1.13.0
  - File > Open Recent: quick access to recently opened/saved scenes (persisted
    between sessions).
  - File > Autosave: saves the open scene every 60s while editing.
  - Unsaved-changes guard: closing the window or choosing Exit with unsaved work
    now prompts to Save / Don't Save / Cancel instead of losing changes.

v1.12.0
  - Pause / Resume while playing (Step now pauses then advances one frame).
  - Play-mode tint: a colored border frames the window while playing (green) or
    paused (amber), so the run state is obvious at a glance.
  - Editor themes: Dark / Light / Classic under View > Theme.
  - View > Clear Console on Play (on by default) wipes the log each run.
  - Status bar shows EDIT / PLAYING / PAUSED.

v1.11.0
  - GameObject > 3D Object submenu to create any built-in primitive (Cube,
    Sphere, Cylinder, Capsule, Cone, Plane, Pyramid, Wedge, Torus, Icosphere,
    Quad).
  - "Fit to mesh" button on 3D Box/Sphere/Capsule colliders sizes them to the
    object's mesh in one click.
  - One-click "Add Physics (Rigidbody + Box)" on a Mesh Renderer adds a
    Rigidbody3D and a box collider fitted to the mesh, so a shape drops and
    collides immediately on Play.

v1.10.0
  - Edit the scene while playing: select and Move/Rotate/Scale objects in the
    Scene view during Play (changes revert on Stop, like Unity).
  - Copy / Paste GameObjects with Ctrl+C / Ctrl+V (also in the GameObject menu).
  - Default skybox: a sky gradient now fills the 3D view background (toggle:
    View > Skybox).
  - IDE: every panel tab now has a close button, and View > Reset Layout
    restores the default docked workspace and reopens all panels.
  - Scripting API expanded a lot: 3D rigidbody control (set_velocity3,
    add_force3, jump…), facing helpers (forward_x/y/z, move_forward/right),
    object identity (name/tag/set_active/destroy_obj), 3D raycast, fps(), and
    more — see Help > Scripting Reference and docs/SCRIPTING.md (both expanded).

v1.9.1
  - Fixed Scene/Game flicker when both 3D views are visible at once (split or
    docked, in or out of Play): the two views shared one render texture and
    fought over it each frame. Each view now renders to its own texture.

v1.9.0
  - Collider gizmos: colliders now draw as green wireframe outlines in the
    Scene view (2D box/circle/capsule and 3D box/sphere/capsule), so you can
    see and size them like Unity. Toggle under View > Colliders (gizmos).

v1.8.0
  - 3D physics: Rigidbody3D (gravity, mass, drag, bounciness, freeze axes) plus
    Box / Sphere / Capsule 3D colliders, with collision response, triggers and
    Raycast / OverlapSphere queries. Stepped automatically each frame.
  - More 2D colliders: added a Capsule Collider 2D (vertical or horizontal).
  - Scene gizmos: cameras now draw a wireframe frustum and lights draw a sun +
    direction arrow, so you can see and place them in the Scene view.
  - Scripting documentation: Help > Scripting Reference (and a Docs button in
    the Script Editor) opens a full OkayScript reference; also in docs/.
  - Scripting is OkayScript-only now (Lua removed); the language picker reflects
    that and the starter script is valid OkayScript so Compile & Run works.

v1.7.0
  - Script editor works now: the starter script is written in the correct
    syntax for the selected language (OkayScript uses C-style braces and
    semicolons, which the previous end-style template broke on).
  - The language picker is real: it lists only the backends this build
    supports, and Lua is now bundled (no external install) so selecting "lua"
    actually runs Lua. Switching language swaps in matching starter syntax.
    (C# needs Mono and isn't available in the standalone Windows build.)

v1.6.0
  - The launcher now checks the engine version on startup and auto-updates in
    the background: if a newer build is published (or a runtime is missing) it
    downloads the engine + player and stages a new launcher, with live status
    in the sidebar and a manual "Check for updates" button. Self-heals the
    "player runtime not found" case by fetching it automatically.
  - Pressing Play in the editor now jumps straight to the Game tab so you see
    your game immediately instead of staying on the Scene view.

v1.5.0
  - Play mode now feeds real keyboard / mouse / gamepad into the engine, so the
    playable templates (Platformer, Top-Down, Coin Collector, Snake) and your
    own movement scripts respond inside the editor — you can actually move now.
  - UI objects (buttons, panels, sliders, toggles, progress bars, images, text)
    now render in the 3D Scene view and the Game view, not just 2D — created UI
    is visible immediately in any project.
  - Build Game is self-contained: if the player runtime isn't sitting next to
    the editor, it's downloaded from GitHub automatically so the exported game
    runs without any manual file copying.
  - The self-updater is back, smarter: Engine > Check for Updates compares your
    version against the published one, shows the release notes, and only offers
    "Download & Install" when a newer build actually exists (no more nagging).
  - Cleaner Inspector header: auto-sized tinted bar with the active toggle, a
    colored type chip, the editable name, Tag field and Save-as-Prefab.

v1.4.0
  - Lighting (Light component + scene shading) and z-buffered solid 3D rendering.
  - Camera scaling locked (move/rotate only) to stop the select-camera flicker.
  - FiveM-style launcher (Create / Play / Marketplace).
